title: Xyridion carthaliniae
taxonomy: Xyridion carthaliniae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Xyridaceae, commonly known as the yellow-eyed grasses. This plant is native to Brazil and is known for its unique characteristics. Xyridion carthaliniae belongs to the genus Xyridion, which consists of several species of flowering plants. The family Xyridaceae is a small but distinct group of plants that are primarily found in the Americas. Within the plant kingdom, Xyridion carthaliniae is classified as an angiosperm, a type of plant that produces flowers and seeds.
